behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index
behaviors.justification: The real failure occurs when the actual embedding model API (e.g., OpenAI text-embedding) returns vectors of a dimensionality that doesn't match the database schema (e.g., expecting 1536 dimensions but receiving 768). A mock embedding service would always return the expected dimension and hide this contract mismatch. You must call the real embedding API to verify its actual behavior and ensure vectors can be stored correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test that sends a natural language query to the actual embedding model API, receives the result, verifies the vector has the expected dimensionality (e.g., 1536 for text-embedding-3-small), and confirms that the returned vectors can be inserted into the real vector database without schema errors. Test multiple query types to ensure consistency.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score
behaviors.justification: The real failure is that the vector database returns results in arbitrary order instead of sorted by similarity. Mocking the search would always return results in whatever order you specify, hiding the failure. You must test against a real vector database (local instance or containerized) with actual vectors inserted to verify that the database engine itself returns ordered results. This tests both the database's behavior and your code's handling of the results.
behaviors.test_description: Insert a set of test vectors into a real vector database instance, perform a similarity search with a known query vector, extract the similarity scores from the results, and verify that scores are in strictly descending order. Repeat with multiple query vectors to ensure consistent ordering across different search scenarios.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on what the real LLM produces—whether it actually understands the nuanced intent and reorders results meaningfully. Mocking the LLM with fixed re-ranked outputs would always pass and wouldn't test whether the actual LLM provides value. The real failure is: the LLM fails to understand context, or the re-ranking provides no improvement. You must call the actual LLM with real queries to verify it produces different rankings. Because LLM output is non-deterministic, you'd run this multiple times to confirm meaningful re-ranking happens.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a query with nuanced intent (e.g., "budget-friendly laptop for gaming" with implicit tradeoff between price and performance), get the raw similarity-ranked results from the vector search, pass these results and the original query to the real LLM re-ranker, and verify that the returned order differs from the original ranking. Run this test multiple times to account for LLM variance and confirm that re-ranking is actually occurring.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise
behaviors.justification: The real failure occurs when a query with no close matches still returns low-similarity results instead of filtering them out. You need a real vector database with real vectors to test this behavior—a mock search API would simply return whatever you tell it to return. The test must insert vectors that represent a specific domain, then search with an out-of-domain query that has no true matches, and verify the similarity threshold correctly filters them out, resulting in an empty set.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a real vector database with vectors from a specific domain (e.g., electronics), then issue a similarity search query for something clearly outside that domain (e.g., a poetry query for an e-commerce search), verify that the raw similarity scores are all below your relevance threshold, and confirm that the final result set is empty (not containing low-relevance items). Vary the threshold to test boundary conditions.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case
behaviors.justification: Mocking the embedding API, vector database, and LLM would return artificially fast results and hide real latency problems. The true failure occurs in production where the real embedding service, vector database, and LLM have variable latency. To catch this failure, you must measure end-to-end latency through all real external services. The LLM adds non-deterministic latency (Agentic component), and network calls add System-level latency; together they require System-level testing to validate the SLA.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end tests with real external services: issue queries through the full pipeline (real embedding API, real vector database search, real LLM re-ranker), measure latency from query receipt to final ranked results returned, and verify that the median latency across a representative set of queries is below 2 seconds. Run multiple iterations to account for variance and network fluctuations, and test with realistic query complexity.
